summary: Critical blockers exist in credential/bootstrap design, JWT secret handling, vulnerable upload dependencies, and deployment/runtime defaults. Production deployment should be blocked until Phase 1 controls are implemented and validated.

findings.title: Default admin credentials exposed and re-applied at startup
findings.description: Known bootstrap admin credentials are present in code/docs/UI and startup logic rewrites master admin password hash on every boot.
findings.impact: Privilege compromise and credential-rotation bypass risk.
findings.evidence: Defaults and forced password_hash update are visible in settings/service code and runtime configuration.
findings.recommended_fix: Remove defaults from code/UI/docs, create one-time bootstrap flow, and stop rewriting admin password during startup.
validation_steps: App fails startup in non-local env when bootstrap credentials are default/weak.
Admin password change persists across restarts.
Login page no longer pre-fills or displays default admin credentials.

findings.title: Weak JWT secret fallback without fail-fast guard
findings.description: JWT secret defaults to a weak value and is accepted unless explicitly overridden.
findings.impact: Potential token forgery and privilege escalation in misconfigured deployments.
findings.evidence: Default `jwt_secret_key` plus direct use for encode/decode.
findings.recommended_fix: Require strong secret policy at startup, rotate keys, and invalidate old tokens.
validation_steps: Startup fails with weak secret in non-local env.
Old tokens are rejected after key rotation.

findings.title: Upload parsing path includes known vulnerable packages and unbounded file reads
findings.description: `pip-audit` reports multiple CVEs in upload/parsing dependencies while endpoints read full files into memory before robust gating.
findings.impact: DoS/resource exhaustion and parser abuse risk.
findings.evidence: pip-audit: 25 vulnerabilities across 4 packages; repeated `await upload_file.read()` patterns.
findings.recommended_fix: Upgrade vulnerable packages and implement strict upload size/type limits plus parser hardening.
validation_steps: pip-audit returns no unresolved high/critical advisories in active path.
Oversized/malformed file uploads are rejected quickly and safely.

findings.title: MongoDB network/auth exposure in compose defaults
findings.description: MongoDB is host-exposed and app URI uses no credentials in defaults.
findings.impact: Unauthorized database read/write risk if network exposure exists.
findings.evidence: Published port 27017 and non-authenticated URI defaults.
findings.recommended_fix: Enable Mongo auth/TLS, remove host port publication in production, and restrict network access.
validation_steps: External network cannot reach MongoDB service.
Unauthenticated DB connection attempts fail.

findings.title: Deployment artifacts run development servers and root users
findings.description: Runtime uses dev commands (`--reload`, Vite dev server) and bind mounts; containers do not set non-root user.
findings.impact: Expanded attack surface and unstable/non-hardened production behavior.
findings.evidence: Docker and compose commands are development-oriented.
findings.recommended_fix: Switch to production build/runtime, remove source mounts in prod, and run as non-root.
validation_steps: Container runs without reload/dev server in production profile.
Container user is non-root and filesystem is hardened.

findings.title: Session token handling and CORS/header hardening gaps
findings.description: Bearer token is stored in localStorage; backend CORS is wide open and header hardening is not visible in app code.
findings.impact: Higher token theft/abuse risk in XSS or origin-misconfiguration scenarios.
findings.evidence: localStorage token persistence and wildcard CORS configuration.
findings.recommended_fix: Prefer HttpOnly secure cookies or enforce strong CSP/XSS controls, and restrict CORS to trusted origins.
validation_steps: CORS rejects unauthorized origins.
Security headers are present at edge/app.
Token cannot be read by arbitrary frontend JS when using cookie strategy.

findings.title: Missing rate limiting on auth and token-sensitive endpoints
findings.description: No endpoint-level throttling/lockout controls are visible for login and reset/invitation workflows.
findings.impact: Credential stuffing, brute force, and abuse-driven DoS risk.
findings.evidence: No rate-limiter middleware/dependency in backend routes.
findings.recommended_fix: Add per-IP and per-identity throttling, login lockout/backoff, and abuse alerts.
validation_steps: Repeated failed login bursts trigger throttling and lockout.
Rate-limit events are logged and alerting is triggered.

findings.title: Path ingestion endpoints permit arbitrary filesystem reads
findings.description: Admin-provided paths are directly read from server filesystem and ingested.
findings.impact: Server-side sensitive file exfiltration if admin access is compromised.
findings.recommended_fix: Disable in production or enforce strict allowlisted root path checks.
validation_steps: Out-of-scope absolute paths are rejected.
Only approved seed directory files are accepted.

findings.title: Internal exception details are disclosed in API responses
findings.description: HTTP error payloads include raw exception strings.
findings.impact: Information disclosure that helps attackers fingerprint internals and error paths.
findings.recommended_fix: Return generic client errors and log full details server-side with trace IDs.
validation_steps: Failure responses no longer include raw parser/upstream exception strings.
Server logs retain actionable details for triage.

findings.title: Frontend advisory chain (vite/esbuild/picomatch) remains vulnerable
findings.description: `npm audit` reports vulnerabilities in toolchain dependencies and runtime currently uses Vite dev server.
findings.impact: Potential dev-server abuse and dependency-level DoS risks.
findings.evidence: npm audit reports 3 vulnerabilities (including high severity in picomatch).
findings.recommended_fix: Upgrade Vite/toolchain chain to patched versions and use production static serving.
validation_steps: npm audit returns no unresolved high/critical vulnerabilities.
Production runtime does not expose Vite dev server.

findings.title: Reset and invitation tokens are plaintext and URL-facing
findings.description: Sensitive one-time tokens are stored/returned plaintext and passed in URL contexts.
findings.impact: Token leakage via logs/history/referrer or datastore compromise.
findings.evidence: Schemas include token fields in API responses and router uses path token for reset context lookup.
findings.recommended_fix: Hash tokens at rest, reduce URL/token exposure, and shorten validity windows.
validation_steps: Database stores only token hashes.
Access logs redact/remove reset/invitation token values.

findings.title: No visible CI/CD security gating
findings.description: Repository does not include tracked CI workflows for security scanning and deployment gating.
findings.impact: Security regressions can reach deployment without automated checks.
findings.evidence: No `.github/workflows` files present in tracked file list.
findings.recommended_fix: Add CI checks for SAST, secret scan, dependency audit, container scan, and policy gates.
validation_steps: PR with intentional vulnerable dependency is blocked by CI policy.
Deployment requires passing security gates and approval workflow.

quick_wins: Remove default admin credentials from frontend and README.
Disable startup password overwrite for master admin account.
Replace wildcard CORS with explicit allowed origins.
Remove `--reload` and Vite dev server from production images.
Implement upload size checks before full file reads.
Replace raw exception details with generic client errors.
Upgrade vulnerable backend and frontend dependencies.
Disable path-based ingestion endpoints in production.
pre_deployment_checklist: Rotate secrets and API keys currently in local runtime env files.
Eliminate default/known credentials from code and deployment config.
Enforce strong JWT secret startup policy.
Upgrade vulnerable dependencies and verify audit cleanliness.
Implement auth endpoint rate limiting and lockout strategy.
Switch containers to production mode and non-root users.
Add CI/CD security gates and release blockers.
post_deployment_checklist: Run staged pen test focused on auth/upload/admin flows.
Verify TLS and security headers at edge.
Confirm token/URL redaction in access and application logs.
Enable alerting for brute-force and upload anomaly patterns.
Establish recurring dependency and image patch cadence.
unknowns: Ingress/reverse-proxy TLS and header policy are not represented in repository files.
Branch protection and deployment approval policy are not visible in repository.
Cloud IAM/storage/network controls are not represented in this codebase.
Runtime log retention/redaction strategy cannot be validated from repository alone.

optimization_targets: Refactor authentication/session lifecycle after hardening baseline is complete.
Optimize file ingestion throughput after adding safe upload guards.
Improve Docker image size and startup time after production runtime migration.
refactor_targets: Split `backend/app/domains/hospitality/service.py` into bounded modules (upload parsing, AI integration, reconciliation, reporting).
Centralize error handling/logging middleware for consistent safe responses.
Introduce a dedicated security config module with environment-specific enforcement.
hardening_targets: Cookie-based auth or hardened token strategy with CSP and strict CORS.
One-time bootstrap admin flow and secret manager integration.
Policy-driven CI/CD security gates and immutable artifact promotion.
tests_to_add: Auth brute-force throttling and lockout tests.
Upload limits and malformed parser regression tests.
Path-ingestion containment tests preventing out-of-root reads.
